name: Google Search Console MCP Server
what_it_does: Google Search Console MCP server enabling AI agents to query SEO performance data from Google Search Console — retrieving search analytics (impressions, clicks, CTR, position), inspecting URL coverage and indexing status, querying performance by query/page/country/device, and integrating GSC's search intelligence into agent-driven SEO analysis and content optimization workflows.
use_cases: Analyzing organic search performance and keyword rankings from SEO agents
Identifying top-performing pages and queries from content strategy agents
Checking URL indexing status and coverage issues from technical SEO agents
Comparing search performance across time periods from analytics agents
Identifying declining pages needing optimization from content audit agents
Analyzing click-through rates for title/description optimization from conversion agents
not_for: Teams not owning verified websites (GSC requires domain ownership verification)
Real-time search ranking data (GSC data has 2-3 day delay)
Competitive SEO analysis (GSC only shows your own site's data)
best_when: An agent needs to analyze organic search performance for verified websites — for SEO optimization, content strategy, or technical search health monitoring.
avoid_when: You need real-time data or competitive analysis — GSC only shows your own sites with 2-3 day data lag. Use Serper/Semrush/Ahrefs for competitive analysis.

auth.notes: Google OAuth 2.0 with Search Console read-only scope (webmasters.readonly). Service account with GSC property access for automation. User must have verified ownership of the GSC property being queried.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Google Search Console API: completely free
pricing.notes: Google Search Console and its API are completely free. No paid tiers for GSC itself. Community MCP server is open source and free.

known_agent_gotchas: Requires Google OAuth setup with Google Cloud project — not simple one-step auth
Domain verification required — GSC property must be verified before API access
Data has 2-3 day processing delay — no real-time search data
Max 16 months of historical data accessible via API
Rows capped at 25,000 per query — must use date/dimension filtering for comprehensive data
Community MCP server — GSC API is straightforward but OAuth setup is the main friction
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Google OAuth 2.0 with webmasters.readonly scope (read-only). GDPR. Community MCP server. Use read-only scope — no modification of GSC properties needed. Store OAuth tokens securely.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Google OAuth 2.0 with read-only scope. GDPR. Community MCP server. Use webmasters.readonly scope. Store OAuth credentials securely.
